stem: A clinical research study measures treatment response rate across 4 trial sites, each containing multiple patient cohorts. trial site A has 15 observations with mean 84.6 % (within-trial site SD = 6.5). trial site B has 10 observations with mean 82.3 % (within-trial site SD = 7.5). trial site C has 20 observations with mean 76.7 % (within-trial site SD = 3.4). trial site D has 10 observations with mean 65.1 % (within-trial site SD = 7.6). The between-trial site standard deviation is uncertain, estimated between 8.3 and 20.9 %. Using a hierarchical Bayesian model, estimate the true treatment response rate for trial site D, accounting for shrinkage toward the grand mean and the uncertainty in the between-trial site variation.

ground_truth.answer: Hierarchical estimate for trial site D: 65.5 % (shrunk from raw 65.1). Range [65.3, 66.1] as between-trial site SD varies from 8.3 to 20.9. Pooled mean: 77.8.

solution_steps: Compute pooled (grand) mean: 77.8 % (weighted average of 4 trial site means).
Shrinkage factor for trial site D: B = σ²_between / (σ²_between + σ²_within/10). At σ_between=13.9: B = 0.971.
Interval [65.3, 66.1] captures uncertainty from the unknown between-group variance.
common_failure_modes: Using the raw observed mean (65.1) without shrinkage
Using only the grand mean (77.8) and ignoring group-specific data
Not recognizing that small-sample groups should be shrunk more toward the grand mean
Treating the between-group variance as known rather than uncertain
